gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: updateTokenPoolInfo missing DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE enforcement allows unprivileged attacker to redirect swap routing to malicious pool
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.sol updateTokenPoolInfo only guards with onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE restriction exists only as NatSpec comment in IPortal.sol and is absent from all implementation files
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged attacker replaces _dexPools entry for a DEX-launched token with a malicious pool address, causing subsequent victim swaps to route through the malicious pool and lose input tokens

live_preconditions.name: at_least_one_dex_launched_token_exists
live_preconditions.observed_value: not confirmed from live_context.json; LaunchedToDEX event is listed in abi_inventory_seed but no decoded event samples are present

live_preconditions.name: global_bit_flag_set_portal_not_halted
live_preconditions.observed_value: not confirmed; bitFlags storage slot not read in live_context.json

live_preconditions.name: dex_pool_entry_for_victim_token_is_writable_via_updateTokenPoolInfo
live_preconditions.observed_value: not confirmed; _dexPools mapping keys not enumerable from live_context.json
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: derive token address from LaunchedToDEX event above, then cast storage 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 $(cast keccak $(cast abi-encode 'f(address)' $TOKEN_ADDR)) --rpc-url $BSC_RPC

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Proxy entry point confirmed to have only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) and no onlyRole or hasRole check; delegates to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER via _delegateToImpl
DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE restriction is a NatSpec comment only (// @dev can only be called by DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE roles); no modifier or require enforces it

source_code_basis.symbols_or_lines: grep-confirmed: updateTokenPoolInfo and _dexPools present (6 matches); onlyRole/hasRole/_checkRole/msg.sender/require.*role/revert.*role absent (0 matches)
source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Implementation delegated to by PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER contains no role enforcement; DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE identifier does not appear anywhere in PortalBase.sol

source_code_basis.symbols_or_lines: 2 matches total, both in IPortal.sol; 0 matches in Portal.sol, PortalBase.sol, PortalCommon.sol
source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Confirms the role identifier is never referenced in any implementation file, ruling out an indirect enforcement path

attacker_path.actor: any unprivileged EOA with no Portal roles
attacker_inputs: token: address of any DEX-launched token with a live _dexPools entry
poolInfo.pool: attacker-deployed contract implementing V4/PCS_INFINITY_CL swap interface
call_sequence: 1. Attacker deploys MaliciousPool implementing the V4/PCS_INFINITY_CL swap interface; records received input tokens; returns 0 output
2. Attacker calls Portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(victimToken, {pool: maliciousPool, poolType: V4, ...}) — passes onlyIfBitFlagsSet, no role check, succeeds
4. PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER reads _dexPools[victimToken], finds poolType==V4, routes swap to maliciousPool
5. MaliciousPool receives victim input tokens and returns 0 output amount
6. Victim receives 0 output; attacker withdraws stolen tokens from MaliciousPool
state_before: bitFlags has CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH bit set (portal active)
state_after: victim's input tokens held in MaliciousPool, claimable by attacker

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: victim's input tokens (BNB, quote tokens, or launched tokens) sent to Portal.swapExactInput
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: full input token amount deposited by victim into the malicious pool
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: victim loses entire swap input; receives 0 output
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: attacker paid nothing and has no role or entitlement to redirect swap routing; NatSpec explicitly requires DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE for this operation

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 against proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Query LaunchedToDEX events to obtain a live victimToken address with a populated _dexPools entry
Confirm bitFlags has CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH set via portal.bitFlags()
Deploy MaliciousV4Pool: implements V4/PCS_INFINITY_CL swap interface, records received tokens, returns 0 amountOut
Fund a victim EOA with the required input token amount
Use a fresh unprivileged EOA as attacker (no roles)
transaction_sequence: vm.prank(attacker); portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(victimToken, PackedDexPool({pool: address(maliciousPool), poolType: PoolType.V4, fee: 500, tickSpacing: 10, unused: 0}));
expected_assertions: assertEq(updateTokenPoolInfo call reverted, false) — call must succeed for unprivileged attacker
assertGt(maliciousPool.stolenBalance(), 0) — malicious pool received victim input tokens
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If updateTokenPoolInfo reverts with an access-control error for the unprivileged attacker, the missing-role-check claim is invalid and the finding must be rejected. If the swap does not route through _dexPools or the malicious pool does not receive tokens, the fund-extraction impact claim is invalid.
